free_text: Measurement and Hybrid-Model Analysis of proton-
induced reactions with V, Fe, and Co
Each stack consisted of high purity metal foils
of the respective element. In front of each stack
a single 25 um Au foil was placed for flux monitoring.
In each stack authors also included foils
of the other target elements for cross checking.
For each target element two types of stacks were
used- one degrading the p-energy from 45 to 25 MeV,
and the other from 30 to 12 MeV - in order to avoid
too large errors of the p-energy due to energy
1332 keV, efficiency 16.5% relative to a 3" x 3"
NaI crystal at a distance of 25 cm).
The efficiency of the detector was determined using
The errors in absolute branching ratios are not
gamma-branching ratios have been considered in the
errors in those cases in which a sufficient number
of undisturbed gamma-lines could be used.

which have been measured on compact cyclotron CV-28,
